blocks.text: Prepared by Mark Todd, Principal Engineer. Original to the personnel file, copy to the employee.

Kimberly Branch joins the firm as Design Technician in the Engineering group. It is written in the same form as the ones before it, which is to say plainly and without a great deal of encouragement in it. She did not ask for any in the interview, and I do not believe she requires it.

The assignment is plan production across the firm's grading, stormwater, roadway, and utility work, drawn to the office CAD standard. She said in the interview that she prefers to understand what a sheet is meant to accomplish before she draws it, and to ask the engineer directly rather than infer it from the markup. That is how it is done here, and it is how it has always been done here. No engineer in this office is entitled to be put out by that question. If one of them is, she should tell me, and I will settle it.

She should be warned about something the standards folder will not tell her. This office has conventions that are written down nowhere: how a particular agency wants its sheets numbered, which detail we substitute for the state standard and the reason we do, what the plotter does on the third sheet if nobody is watching it. Those conventions live in the heads of the people who have been here a while, and they will be handed to her one at a time and out of order, usually at the moment they are needed and not before. My advice, and it is advice rather than instruction, is that she write each one down as she collects it. Whatever she builds that way will belong to her, and I expect she will find it worth more than the standards folder is.

Two rules to close on. Time is booked to a job number the day it is worked, and drawing files stay in the lettered project folder on the share. And the name of a public agency is spelled correctly in the title block, every time, checked against the executed agreement and not against memory. This firm has never sent a set out the door with a client's name wrong on the cover. I would like that record to survive the arrival of Kimberly Branch. I say the same thing to everyone, so she should read nothing into it.
